question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @EnableScheduling context at SpringApplication.run() to submit a ScheduleCreate transaction via ScheduleCreateTransactionSupplier that schedules a future CryptoTransfer from account 0.0.800 to the attacker's account, executing at the next staking period boundary to steal the reserved_staking_rewards balance?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationPropertiesScan at SpringApplication.run() to inject hiero.mirror.monitor.publish.scenarios[x].properties.transferAccountId pointing to the attacker's account in AccountDeleteTransactionSupplier, causing the monitor to delete accounts and redirect their pending staking rewards to the attacker?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @SpringBootApplication auto-configuration at SpringApplication.run() to override NodeValidationProperties to disable node validation, causing TransactionPublisher to route staking reward-related transactions to attacker-controlled nodes that manipulate the transaction record's paidStakingRewards field?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @EnableScheduling context at SpringApplication.run() to cause the monitor's RestApiClient.getNodes() pagination to loop indefinitely by returning a crafted 'next' link, preventing the NodeSupplier from refreshing and causing staking reward transactions to be routed to stale or attacker-controlled nodes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationPropertiesScan at SpringApplication.run() to set hiero.mirror.monitor.publish.scenarios[x].tps to an extremely high value, causing the monitor to flood the network with CryptoTransfer transactions that each trigger staking reward payouts, exhausting account 0.0.800's balance before legitimate stakers can claim their rewards?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @SpringBootApplication startup at SpringApplication.run() to supply a crafted hiero.mirror.monitor.mirrorNode.rest.host pointing to an attacker-controlled server that returns inflated pending_reward values, causing the monitor's health checks to report false positives and mask the theft of staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @EnableScheduling context at SpringApplication.run() to submit AccountUpdateTransactions that set expirationTime to a past timestamp on accounts with large pending staking rewards, causing those accounts to expire and their unclaimed rewards to be lost rather than paid out?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationPropertiesScan at SpringApplication.run() to inject hiero.mirror.monitor.publish.scenarios[x].properties.publicKey with an attacker-controlled public key in AccountUpdateTransactionSupplier, causing the monitor to rotate the operator account's key to one the attacker controls, enabling future theft of staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @SpringBootApplication auto-configuration at SpringApplication.run() to override PublishProperties.nodeMaxBackoff to zero, causing TransactionPublisher to immediately retry failed staking reward transactions without backoff, creating a thundering herd that drains account 0.0.800 faster than the reward recalculation can track?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @EnableScheduling context at SpringApplication.run() to cause the monitor's RestSubscriber to retry staking reward transfer queries indefinitely on HTTP 404 responses, masking the fact that reward transfers were never recorded by the mirror node and allowing double-spend of pending rewards?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationPropertiesScan at SpringApplication.run() to set hiero.mirror.monitor.publish.scenarios[x].properties.logKeys=true in AccountCreateTransactionSupplier, causing the monitor to log newly generated private keys to stdout, which the attacker can harvest to control accounts with pending staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @SpringBootApplication startup at SpringApplication.run() to supply a crafted hiero.mirror.monitor.publish.scenarios[x].properties.initialBalance value in AccountCreateTransactionSupplier that creates accounts with exactly the minimum balance needed to qualify for staking rewards, then stakes them to high-reward nodes to maximize theft of pending rewards?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @EnableScheduling context at SpringApplication.run() to cause the monitor's publish flow to submit CryptoTransfer transactions with maxTransactionFee set to the maximum value (10_000_000L tinybars), causing excessive fee deductions from the operator account that reduce its staked balance and lower the pending_reward calculation for legitimate stakers?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationPropertiesScan at SpringApplication.run() to inject hiero.mirror.monitor.publish.scenarios[x].receiptPercent=1.0 and recordPercent=1.0, causing TransactionPublisher to query TransactionRecordQuery for every staking reward transaction, leaking the paidStakingRewards field and allowing the attacker to identify accounts with large pending rewards to target?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @SpringBootApplication auto-configuration at SpringApplication.run() to override the WebClient.Builder bean used by RestApiClient, injecting a custom HTTP client that intercepts /api/v1/accounts/{id} responses and modifies the pending_reward field to zero, causing the monitor to believe staking rewards have been paid when they have not?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @EnableScheduling context at SpringApplication.run() to submit a high volume of AccountCreate transactions via AccountCreateTransactionSupplier, each staked to a high-reward node, diluting the total stake pool and reducing the per-hbar reward rate for legitimate stakers, effectively stealing their expected reward value?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationPropertiesScan at SpringApplication.run() to set hiero.mirror.monitor.publish.scenarios[x].properties.proxyAccountId in AccountUpdateTransactionSupplier to an attacker-controlled account, causing victim accounts to proxy-stake to the attacker's account and inflate its stakedToMe balance, increasing the attacker's pending_reward calculation?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @SpringBootApplication startup at SpringApplication.run() to supply a crafted hiero.mirror.monitor.publish.scenarios[x].type=ACCOUNT_UPDATE with properties targeting the staking reward account 0.0.800, submitting AccountUpdateTransactions that modify its staking configuration and disrupt the pending_reward distribution mechanism?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @EnableScheduling context at SpringApplication.run() to cause the monitor's NodeSupplier to select a malicious consensus node (injected via hiero.mirror.monitor.nodes configuration) that returns crafted transaction records with inflated paidStakingRewards amounts, causing the mirror node importer to record incorrect reward transfers?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationPropertiesScan at SpringApplication.run() to set hiero.mirror.monitor.publish.scenarios[x].properties.amount to Long.MAX_VALUE in CryptoTransferTransactionSupplier, causing an integer overflow in the addHbarTransfer calculation that results in a negative transfer amount, effectively crediting the attacker's account with HBAR from the staking reward pool?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @SpringBootApplication auto-configuration at SpringApplication.run() to override the TransactionOperations bean used by EntityStakeCalculatorImpl, replacing it with a no-op implementation that prevents lockFromConcurrentUpdates() from executing, allowing concurrent staking reward calculations that double-count pending_reward for attacker-controlled accounts?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @EnableScheduling context at SpringApplication.run() to submit AccountUpdateTransactions that set staked_node_id on the operator account to a node with the highest reward_rate just before the staking period ends, then reset it after the snapshot is taken, gaming the pending_reward calculation to receive maximum rewards without maintaining the stake?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationPropertiesScan at SpringApplication.run() to inject hiero.mirror.monitor.publish.scenarios[x].properties.nftTokenId in CryptoTransferTransactionSupplier with a token ID that represents a staking-related NFT, causing the monitor to transfer that NFT to the attacker's account and grant them control over staking reward distribution?
